channel.title: CyberNetSec - Policy and Compliance
channel.description: Latest policy and compliance threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: Supply Chain Attacks Hit 26% of UK Businesses in the Last Year
item.description:
A survey by Databarracks reveals that one in four (26%) UK businesses have suffered a cyber incident originating from their supply chain in the past 12 months. The 'Data Health Check 2026' report highlights a concerning trend: 48% of IT decision-makers admit to knowingly working with suppliers that ...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Governments Move Toward Banning Ransomware Payments Amid Rising Attacks
item.description:
As ransomware attacks become more frequent and sophisticated, governments globally are considering and implementing bans on ransom payments to cybercriminals. A July 20, 2026 report highlights a planned U.K. ban for public sector and critical infrastructure organizations as a key example of this gro...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: Report: Enterprises Overwhelmed by Disjointed Security Tools Amid AI Risks
item.description:
A report from Thales reveals that enterprises are using a multitude of separate security tools, leading to policy inconsistencies and visibility gaps. This 'tool sprawl,' where organizations use an average of seven different tools for data protection alone, is creating a significant and expanding at...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Cloud Security, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: White House Launches 'Gold Eagle' AI-Powered Vulnerability Hub
item.description:
The White House has officially launched 'Gold Eagle,' a new national cybersecurity initiative designed to serve as a central clearinghouse for software vulnerabilities. The program will leverage artificial intelligence to discover, prioritize, and coordinate the remediation of security flaws, partic...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Canada Warns Banks of AI Risks from Anthropic's Claude Mythos
item.description:
Canada's financial regulator, the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI), has issued a warning to the country's major banks and insurers about the cybersecurity threats posed by advanced AI models, specifically citing Anthropic's Claude Mythos. In an April 2026 confidential al...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Cloud Security, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: EU & UK Sanction Russian GRU, FSB Officers in Joint Cyber Action
item.description:
The European Union and the United Kingdom have implemented their first joint sanctions package against Russian cyber operations, targeting 24 individuals and entities. The sanctions name senior officers from Russia's GRU and FSB intelligence services, formally attributing attacks on Poland's energy ...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: WatchGuard: Shadow AI, Password Reuse Fueling Business Cyber Risk
item.description:
A July 2026 report from WatchGuard Technologies finds that employee behaviors are creating significant, unmanaged cybersecurity risks for businesses. The study reveals a pervasive "shadow AI" problem, with 64% of employees using unauthorized AI tools for work. This is compounded by persistent poor s...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Phishing, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: AI Increases Need for Human Oversight in Cyber Roles, ISC2 Finds
item.description:
A new report from ISC2 finds that artificial intelligence is transforming cybersecurity roles by increasing the need for human validation and governance, rather than simply replacing jobs. The study, released July 14, 2026, shows that 63% of cybersecurity professionals now spend more time reviewing ...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: US Sanctions VPN Provider 1VPNS for Enabling Ransomware Gangs
item.description:
The U.S. Treasury Department has sanctioned First VPN Service (1VPNS), its Ukrainian administrator Dmytro Rashevskyi, and a Belarusian malware 'cryptor' seller, Yegeniy Silayev, for their roles in facilitating ransomware attacks. Announced on July 13, 2026, the sanctions target 1VPNS for providing a...

Severity: High

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Threat Actor, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: NSA Warns of Russian FSB Targeting Vulnerable Routers in Critical Infrastructure
item.description:
The U.S. National Security Agency (NSA), along with CISA, the FBI, and international partners from the 'Five Eyes' alliance, has issued a joint advisory warning that Russia's Federal Security Service (FSB) is actively targeting vulnerable network routers. The campaign, attributed to FSB Center 16 (a...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Cyberattack, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →
